Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine, low total CO₂, and marked proteinuria, suggesting impaired renal function with possible metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals glucosuria, ketonuria, acidic pH, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, raising suspicion for diabetic ketoacidosis or another metabolic disturbance, despite a low HbA1c. Immediate clinical evaluation for acute or chronic kidney disease and metabolic derangements is recommended.
